data.content:
### Stop Event Hookify Rule with Completion Checklist

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/hookify/skills/writing-rules/SKILL.md

Implements hookify rules triggered when the agent attempts to stop, typically used for enforcing completion requirements. Provides checklists and reminders about required steps before allowing session termination.

```markdown
---
event: stop
pattern: .*
---

Before stopping, verify:
- [ ] Tests were run
- [ ] Build succeeded
- [ ] Documentation updated
```

--------------------------------

### Platform Detection and Conditional Logic in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/marketplace-considerations.md

Detects the operating system (macOS, Linux, Windows) using uname and sets platform-specific variables like PATH_SEP and NULL_DEVICE. Enables cross-platform commands by adapting behavior based on detected platform. Essential for creating truly portable commands that work across different environments.

```bash
case "$(uname)" in
  Darwin*)  PLATFORM="macOS" ;;
  Linux*)   PLATFORM="Linux" ;;
  MINGW*|MSYS*|CYGWIN*) PLATFORM="Windows" ;;
  *)        PLATFORM="Unknown" ;;
esac

Platform: $PLATFORM

if [ "$PLATFORM" = "Windows" ]; then
  PATH_SEP="\\"
  NULL_DEVICE="NUL"
else
  PATH_SEP="/"
  NULL_DEVICE="/dev/null"
fi
```

--------------------------------

### Shell - Load User Configuration with Defaults

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/marketplace-considerations.md

Loads user-specific configuration from a local markdown file with YAML frontmatter, falling back to sensible defaults. Parses settings like verbose mode, color output, and result limits from .claude/plugin-name.local.md if it exists.

```shell
if [ -f ".claude/plugin-name.local.md" ]; then
  # Parse YAML frontmatter for settings
  VERBOSE=$(grep "^verbose:" .claude/plugin-name.local.md | cut -d: -f2 | tr -d ' ')
  COLOR=$(grep "^color:" .claude/plugin-name.local.md | cut -d: -f2 | tr -d ' ')
  MAX_RESULTS=$(grep "^max_results:" .claude/plugin-name.local.md | cut -d: -f2 | tr -d ' ')

  echo "✓ Using user configuration"
else
  echo "Using default configuration"
  echo "Create .claude/plugin-name.local.md to customize"
fi
```

--------------------------------

### Cancel Ralph Loop Command Usage (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/ralph-wiggum/commands/help.md

The `/cancel-ralph` command is used to terminate an active Ralph loop. It removes the state file that maintains the loop's context, providing a clean way to stop the iterative process.

```bash
/cancel-ralph
```

--------------------------------

### Handle Missing Arguments with Conditional Logic in SlashCommand

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/SKILL.md

Provides an example of using conditional logic (`$IF`) within a SlashCommand's markdown to check if an argument is provided. If the argument is missing, it displays a usage message, ensuring robustness and guiding the user on correct command invocation.

```markdown
---
argument-hint: [pr-number]
---

$IF($1,
  Review PR #$1,
  Please provide a PR number. Usage: /review-pr [number]
)
```

--------------------------------

### Edit Plugin Settings and Restart Claude Code

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/examples/example-settings.md

Workflow for manually editing plugin configuration files and applying changes through restart. Requires explicit Claude Code process termination and restart to reload configuration since hooks cannot be hot-swapped during runtime.

```bash
# Edit settings
vim .claude/my-plugin.local.md

# Changes take effect after restart
exit  # Exit Claude Code
claude  # Restart
```

--------------------------------

### Check Completion Promise and Exit Ralph Loop

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/references/real-world-examples.md

Validates if a completion promise has been detected in the last output using regex extraction from XML tags. If matched, removes the state file and exits successfully. This pattern demonstrates promise-based loop termination with proper exit signaling.

```bash
if [[ "$COMPLETION_PROMISE" != "null" ]] && [[ -n "$COMPLETION_PROMISE" ]]; then
  PROMISE_TEXT=$(echo "$LAST_OUTPUT" | perl -0777 -pe 's/.*?<promise>(.*?)<\/promise>.*/$1/s; s/^\s+|\s+$//g')

  if [[ "$PROMISE_TEXT" = "$COMPLETION_PROMISE" ]]; then
    echo "✅ Ralph loop: Detected completion"
    rm "$RALPH_STATE_FILE"
    exit 0
  fi
fi
```

--------------------------------

### First-Run Initialization and Onboarding in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/marketplace-considerations.md

Implements first-run experience by checking for initialization marker and displaying welcome message, feature explanation, and quick start guide. Creates persistent state to avoid repeated onboarding while providing clear guidance for new users.

```bash
if [ ! -f ".claude/command-initialized" ]; then
  echo "Welcome to Command Name!"
  echo ""
  echo "This appears to be your first time using this command."
  echo ""
  echo "WHAT THIS COMMAND DOES:"
  echo "[Brief explanation of purpose and benefits]"
  echo ""
  echo "QUICK START:"
  echo "1. Basic usage: /command [arg]"
  echo "2. For help: /command help"
  echo "3. Examples: /command examples"
  echo ""
  echo "SETUP:"
  echo "No additional setup required. You're ready to go!"
  echo ""
  mkdir -p .claude
  touch .claude/command-initialized
fi
```

--------------------------------

### Perform Automated PR Code Review with /code-review

Source: https://context7.com/anthropics/claude-code/llms.txt

This slash command initiates an automated code review for the current pull request, leveraging multiple specialized AI agents. It can output the review directly to the terminal or post it as a comment on the pull request, identifying issues like CLAUDE.md compliance, bugs, and context discrepancies.

```bash
/code-review
```

```bash
/code-review --comment
```

--------------------------------

### Type Check TypeScript Projects with `npx tsc --noEmit`

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/agent-sdk-dev/README.md

Regularly run this command in TypeScript projects to perform type checking without generating JavaScript output. This ensures code quality and helps catch type-related issues early in the development cycle. It relies on `tsc` being available via `npx`.

```bash
npx tsc --noEmit
```

--------------------------------

### Bash Commands for Monitoring Claude Code Resource Usage

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/testing-strategies.md

This snippet provides Bash commands to monitor the system resources (memory, CPU, process count) consumed by the Claude Code application during command execution. It uses `claude --debug` in one terminal and `watch 'ps aux | grep claude'` in another for real-time observation.

```bash
# Monitor Claude Code during command execution
# In terminal 1:
claude --debug

# In terminal 2:
watch -n 1 'ps aux | grep claude'

# Execute command and observe:
# - Memory usage
# - CPU usage
# - Process count
```

--------------------------------

### Specify Claude Model in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

These YAML examples illustrate how to specify which Claude model (`haiku`, `sonnet`, `opus`) should execute a slash command. This field allows tailoring the model's capability to the command's complexity, optimizing for speed, cost, or reasoning power. If not specified, the model inherits from the conversation context.

```yaml
model: haiku    # Fast, efficient for simple tasks
```

```yaml
model: sonnet   # Balanced performance (default)
```

```yaml
model: opus     # Maximum capability for complex tasks
```

--------------------------------

### Cross-Platform Clipboard Access in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/marketplace-considerations.md

Provides platform-agnostic clipboard functionality by detecting available clipboard tools (pbcopy, xclip, clip.exe) and falling back gracefully. Replaces platform-specific commands with universal detection logic to ensure commands work across macOS, Linux, and Windows.

```bash
if command -v pbcopy > /dev/null; then
  pbcopy < file.txt
elif command -v xclip > /dev/null; then
  xclip -selection clipboard < file.txt
elif command -v clip.exe > /dev/null; then
  cat file.txt | clip.exe
else
  echo "Clipboard not available on this platform"
fi
```

--------------------------------

### Enable Specific Bash Command Output in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

This YAML example shows how to enable a command to execute and retrieve output from specific Bash commands, like `git status:*` and `git diff:*`. This fine-grained control allows for specific shell interactions while maintaining security by limiting the scope of executable Bash commands. It's useful for commands needing to report on specific system states.

```yaml
allowed-tools: Bash(git status:*), Bash(git diff:*)
```

--------------------------------

### Combine Positional and Remaining Arguments in SlashCommand

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/SKILL.md

Shows how to mix positional arguments with the capture of remaining arguments in a SlashCommand prompt. The first two arguments are treated positionally, while the third placeholder ('$3') captures all subsequent input as a single string, offering flexible command design.

```markdown
Deploy $1 to $2 environment with options: $3
```

--------------------------------

### Capture Individual Positional Arguments ($1, $2, etc.) in SlashCommand

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/SKILL.md

Illustrates how to capture individual arguments based on their position using '$1', '$2', '$3', and so on within a SlashCommand's prompt. This allows for structured input where different arguments map to specific parts of the command's logic or output.

```markdown
---
description: Review PR with priority and assignee
argument-hint: [pr-number] [priority] [assignee]
---

Review pull request #$1 with priority level $2.
After review, assign to $3 for follow-up.
```

--------------------------------

### Organize SlashCommands with a Flat Directory Structure

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/SKILL.md

Illustrates a flat directory structure for organizing SlashCommands, where all command files reside directly under a single parent directory. This simple approach is suitable for projects with a small to medium number of commands that do not require complex categorization.

```plaintext
.claude/commands/
├── build.md
├── test.md
├── deploy.md
├── review.md
└── docs.md
```

--------------------------------

### Check for Software Updates and Display Notification (Shell)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/marketplace-considerations.md

A shell script snippet that compares the current application version against the latest available version. If an update is detected, it outputs a detailed notification including the new version, current version, a summary of changes, and instructions for updating the plugin.

```shell
if [ "$CURRENT_VERSION" != "$LATEST_VERSION" ]; then
  📢 UPDATE AVAILABLE

  New version: $LATEST_VERSION
  Current: $CURRENT_VERSION

  What's new:
  - Feature improvements
  - Bug fixes
  - Performance enhancements

  Update with:
    /plugin update plugin-name

  Release notes: https://releases.example.com/v$LATEST_VERSION
fi
```

--------------------------------

### SlashCommand with Multiple Arguments and Hints

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Complete SlashCommand example demonstrating multiple arguments with descriptive names in argument-hint. The body references each argument using positional parameters ($1, $2, $3) corresponding to the hint order.

```markdown
---
description: Deploy to environment
argument-hint: [app-name] [environment] [version]
---

Deploy $1 to $2 using version $3...
```

--------------------------------

### SlashCommand with Single Argument Hint

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Complete SlashCommand example with frontmatter and body demonstrating argument-hint usage for a simple command. Shows how argument-hint integrates with description and command body using positional parameter references ($1).

```markdown
---
description: Fix issue by number
argument-hint: [issue-number]
---

Fix issue #$1...
```

--------------------------------

### Use Environment Variables Instead of Hardcoded Paths

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/examples/plugin-commands.md

Demonstrates why hardcoding absolute file paths breaks plugin portability across different user installations and systems. Using ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} ensures the plugin works regardless of installation location.

```markdown
# Wrong - breaks on different installations
@/home/user/.claude/plugins/my-plugin/config.json

# Correct - works everywhere
@${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/config.json
```

--------------------------------

### Capture All Arguments as a Single String ($ARGUMENTS) in SlashCommand

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/SKILL.md

Demonstrates how to capture all subsequent user-provided arguments into a single string using the '$ARGUMENTS' placeholder within a SlashCommand definition. This is useful for commands where the entire input after the command acts as one cohesive message or identifier.

```markdown
---
description: Fix issue by number
argument-hint: [issue-number]
---

Fix issue #$ARGUMENTS following our coding standards and best practices.
```

--------------------------------

### Update Single Field in Frontmatter Atomically in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/references/parsing-techniques.md

Demonstrates how to modify a single field (e.g., incrementing a counter) within the frontmatter using `sed`. It integrates the atomic update pattern to ensure the file is updated safely and without risk of data loss.

```bash
# Increment iteration counter
CURRENT=$(echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep '^iteration:' | sed 's/iteration: *//')
NEXT=$((CURRENT + 1))

# Update file
TEMP_FILE="${FILE}.tmp.$$"
sed "s/^iteration: .*/iteration: $NEXT/" "$FILE" > "$TEMP_FILE"
mv "$TEMP_FILE" "$FILE"
```

--------------------------------

### Claude Command: Deploy to a Specified Environment

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/examples/simple-commands.md

This command defines a 'deploy' action for Claude to deploy a specific version to a target environment. It takes environment and version as arguments, performs pre-deployment checks, outlines deployment steps using 'kubectl' via 'Bash', and requires a rollback plan.

```markdown
---
description: Deploy to specified environment
argument-hint: [environment] [version]
allowed-tools: Bash(kubectl:*), Read
---

Deploy to $1 environment using version $2

**Pre-deployment Checks:**
1. Verify $1 configuration exists
2. Check version $2 is valid
3. Verify cluster accessibility: !`kubectl cluster-info`

**Deployment Steps:**
1. Update deployment manifest with version $2
2. Apply configuration to $1
3. Monitor rollout status
4. Verify pod health
5. Run smoke tests

**Rollback Plan:**
Document current version for rollback if issues occur.

Proceed with deployment? (yes/no)
```

```bash
> /deploy staging v1.2.3
```

--------------------------------

### Reference Static Files in a SlashCommand Prompt

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/SKILL.md

Shows how to include the content of known, static files directly into a SlashCommand prompt without relying on arguments. This is useful for providing fixed contextual information, such as configuration files, for analysis or consistency checks by Claude.

```markdown
Review @package.json and @tsconfig.json for consistency

Ensure:
- TypeScript version matches
- Dependencies are aligned
- Build configuration is correct
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Haiku Model for Simple Tasks in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

This YAML snippet demonstrates configuring the `haiku` model for commands that are simple, formulaic, and require fast execution. Using `haiku` optimizes for speed and efficiency, making it suitable for low-complexity tasks or frequent invocations where quick responses are paramount. It's often paired with a brief description.

```yaml
---
description: Format code file
model: haiku
---
```

--------------------------------

### Feature Detection and Graceful Degradation in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/marketplace-considerations.md

Detects optional tools (gh, docker) at runtime and adapts command behavior to available features. Provides full functionality with optional dependencies while offering reduced but functional behavior when tools are missing, with helpful suggestions for installation.

```bash
FEATURES=""

if command -v gh > /dev/null; then
  FEATURES="$FEATURES github"
fi

if command -v docker > /dev/null; then
  FEATURES="$FEATURES docker"
fi

echo "Available features: $FEATURES"

if echo "$FEATURES" | grep -q "github"; then
  echo "GitHub integration available"
else
  echo "Limited functionality: GitHub CLI not installed"
  echo "Install 'gh' for full features"
fi
```

--------------------------------

### Set User Environment Variables for stdio Server Credentials

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/mcp-integration/references/authentication.md

This Bash snippet illustrates how a user would set environment variables in their shell to provide credentials for a stdio server. These variables, such as `DATABASE_URL`, `DB_USER`, and `DB_PASSWORD`, are then picked up by the Claude Code plugin's configuration for authentication.

```bash
# User sets these in their shell
export DATABASE_URL="postgresql://localhost/mydb"
export DB_USER="myuser"
export DB_PASSWORD="mypassword"
```

--------------------------------

### Load Claude Plugin from Local Directory (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/commands/create-plugin.md

This command starts the Claude Code development environment ('cc'), instructing it to load a plugin from a specified local file path. This is crucial for local testing and development without formal installation, allowing developers to iterate quickly on their plugins.

```bash
cc --plugin-dir /path/to/plugin-name
```

--------------------------------

### Invalid Model Name - Corrected Format

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Shows correct Claude model name specification. Valid options are sonnet, opus, or haiku for the model property in SlashCommand configuration.

```yaml
model: sonnet
```

--------------------------------

### Complex SlashCommand with All Common Fields

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Comprehensive SlashCommand example demonstrating all common configuration fields: description, argument-hint, allowed-tools with multiple tool specifications, and model selection. Includes embedded commands and structured instructions.

```markdown
---
description: Deploy application to environment
argument-hint: [app-name] [environment] [version]
allowed-tools: Bash(kubectl:*), Bash(helm:*), Read
model: sonnet
---

Deploy $1 to $2 environment using version $3

Pre-deployment checks:
- Verify $2 configuration
- Check cluster status: !`kubectl cluster-info`
- Validate version $3 exists

Proceed with deployment following deployment runbook.
```

--------------------------------

### Allow All Tools in YAML Frontmatter (Not Recommended)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

This YAML example illustrates how to grant a slash command access to all available tools using the wildcard `*`. While providing maximum flexibility, this configuration is generally not recommended due to significant security implications, as it removes all tool-based restrictions. It should be used with extreme caution.

```yaml
allowed-tools: "*"
```

--------------------------------

### Restrict Command to Read-Only Operations in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

This YAML configuration restricts a slash command to perform only read-only operations using `Read, Grep`. By explicitly limiting tool access, this ensures the command cannot modify any data or system state, significantly enhancing security. This is a crucial practice for commands intended for inspection or information retrieval.

```yaml
allowed-tools: Read, Grep  # Read-only command
```

--------------------------------

### Shell - Deprecation Warning Handler

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/marketplace-considerations.md

Detects usage of deprecated command flags and displays migration guidance. Warns users about deprecation timelines and provides examples of how to use new alternatives while maintaining backward compatibility during the deprecation period.

```shell
if [ "$1" = "--old-flag" ]; then
  echo "⚠️  DEPRECATION WARNING"
  echo ""
  echo "The --old-flag option is deprecated as of v2.0.0"
  echo "It will be removed in v3.0.0 (est. June 2025)"
  echo ""
  echo "Use instead: --new-flag"
  echo ""
  echo "Example:"
  echo "  Old: /command --old-flag value"
  echo "  New: /command --new-flag value"
  echo ""
  echo "See migration guide: /command migrate"
  echo ""
  echo "Continuing with deprecated behavior for now..."
fi
```

--------------------------------

### Execute Deployment to Environment (Markdown/Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/advanced-workflows.md

This command reads the current deployment state, executes the main deployment script (`deploy.sh`) for the specified environment, updates the state to 'completed', and then suggests running the cleanup command to finalize the deployment workflow.

```markdown
---
description: Execute deployment
allowed-tools: Read, Bash, Write
---

Reading state: @.claude/deployment-state.local.md

Executing deployment to [environment]...

!`deploy.sh [environment]`

Deployment complete.
Updating state to 'completed'...

Cleanup: /deployment-cleanup
```

--------------------------------

### Migrate Bash String Contains Check to Natural Language Prompt

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/hook-development/references/migration.md

Demonstrates pattern for converting a bash script that checks for privilege escalation keywords (sudo) into a natural language prompt. Shows before and after approaches for improved maintainability and flexibility.

```bash
if [[ "$command" == *"sudo"* ]]; then
  echo "Privilege escalation" >&2
  exit 2
fi
```

```text
"Check for privilege escalation (sudo, su, etc)"
```

--------------------------------

### Shell - Diagnostic Information Collection

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/marketplace-considerations.md

Collects diagnostic information to help debug operation failures. Gathers environment details including platform, shell, working directory, and checks for common issues like git repository status, write permissions, and required files. Output can be included in support requests.

```shell
Environment:
- Platform: $(uname)
- Shell: $SHELL
- Working directory: $(pwd)
- Command: /command $@

Checking common issues:
- Git repository: $(git rev-parse --git-dir 2>&1)
- Write permissions: $(test -w . && echo "OK" || echo "DENIED")
- Required files: $(test -f config.yml && echo "Found" || echo "Missing")
```

--------------------------------

### User Input Validation with Typo Detection in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/marketplace-considerations.md

Handles common user mistakes by detecting typos (hlep, hepl for help) and suggesting similar valid options when invalid input is provided. Improves user experience by being forgiving and helpful rather than simply rejecting invalid input.

```bash
if [ "$1" = "hlep" ] || [ "$1" = "hepl" ]; then
  echo "Did you mean: help?"
  echo ""
  echo "Showing help instead..."
  exit 0
fi

if [ "$1" != "valid-option1" ] && [ "$1" != "valid-option2" ]; then
  echo "Unknown option: $1"
  echo ""
  echo "Did you mean:"
  echo "- valid-option1 (most similar)"
  echo "- valid-option2"
  echo ""
  echo "For all options: /command help"
  exit 1
fi
```

--------------------------------

### Manual-Only Command with disable-model-invocation

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

SlashCommand configured for manual-only execution with disable-model-invocation set to true. Example demonstrates preventing automated invocation for commands requiring user judgment, such as production deployment approvals.

```markdown
---
description: Approve deployment to production
disable-model-invocation: true
---
```

--------------------------------

### Specify Multiple Allowed Tools (Array) in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

This YAML snippet shows how to define multiple allowed tools for a slash command using a list (array) format. This approach provides a clear and structured way to specify various tools, including specialized ones like `Bash(git:*)`, enhancing readability and maintainability of the frontmatter configuration. It's an alternative to the comma-separated string format.

```yaml
allowed-tools:
  - Read
  - Write
  - Bash(git:*)
```

--------------------------------

### Validate Command Output in Claude Commands (Markdown, Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/plugin-features-reference.md

Demonstrates how to build a project and subsequently validate its output. This command executes a build script and then checks the exit code, the existence of a 'dist' directory, and the count of files within it using Bash commands.

```markdown
---
description: Build and validate output
allowed-tools: Bash(*)
---

Build: !`bash ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/build.sh`

Validate output:
- Exit code: !`echo $?`
- Output exists: !`test -d dist && echo "✓" || echo "✗"`
- File count: !`find dist -type f | wc -l`

Report build status and any validation failures.
```
